messages.content:
Dear Yilei,
I wanted to give you an update, since you had first applied to Sakana AI earlier.
As I had mentioned before: _But as we had mentioned earlier, we will be evaluating the solutions as we receive them from candidates, whenever they come, so just keep that in mind._
As such, due to recent hiring, we currently don't have openings for the Member of Technical Staff (e.g. researchers) anymore.
If you are interested, we have openings for the Software Engineer (Research and Development) role on our website, which we encourage you to apply for, if interested:
https://sakana.ai/careers/#software-engineer-research-and-development
I think it might actually be a great fit for you. If you are interested, please feel free to reapply for this role from scratch again.
In addition, we have openings for Applied Research Engineer and for the regular Software Engineering position (Japanese language requirement):
https://sakana.ai/careers/#applied-research-engineer
https://sakana.ai/careers/#software-engineer
Best,
Fish
  
用英文帮我回复一下：说我之前在过中国新年假期，忘记回复了抱歉。我11月找到了一家中国国内公司的实习，考虑到入职周期以及项目兴趣匹配，我就迅速入职了。之后就没有时间做Sakana的题目了。我对Sakana AI一直都有兴趣，我有空会先做题目发给你们的（即使目前没有岗位opening），我也在积极学日语。期待未来能入职Sakana AI。感谢你们对我的兴趣

Here are a few options for your reply, ranging from standard professional to a slightly more conversational tone (which often works well with startups/tech companies).

### Option 1: Professional & Clear (Recommended)
This version clearly explains your timeline and keeps the door open professionally.

**Subject:** Re: Update regarding your application to Sakana AI

Dear Fish,

Thank you for the update and for thinking of me for the Software Engineer role.

Please accept my apologies for the delay in replying; I was recently away for the Chinese New Year holidays.

Regarding my status: back in November, I received an internship offer from a tech company here in China. Considering the hiring timeline and the strong alignment with my project interests at the time, I decided to accept the offer and start immediately. As a result, I unfortunately haven't had the bandwidth to complete the Sakana AI coding assignment recently.

However, I have maintained a strong interest in Sakana AI throughout this time. Even though there are no immediate openings for the Researcher role, I plan to complete the assignment in my spare time and share it with you once finished, just for your reference.

I am also actively learning Japanese to better prepare myself for future opportunities. I truly hope to have the chance to join Sakana AI in the future.

Thank you again for your interest and for keeping in touch.

Best regards,

Yilei

***

### Option 2: Slightly more casual & Enthusiastic
Use this if you want to sound a bit more personal.

**Subject:** Re: Update regarding your application to Sakana AI

Dear Fish,

Thanks so much for reaching out and for the suggestion regarding the Software Engineer position.

So sorry for the slow response—I just got back from the Chinese New Year break.

To give you a quick update on my side: I actually started an internship at a company in China back in November. The project match was great and the onboarding process was very fast, so I jumped into it quickly. Because of this new workload, I wasn't able to find the time to properly work on the Sakana AI entrance challenge.

That said, I am still very enthusiastic about Sakana AI. I intend to finish the challenge/questions whenever I have some free time and send them over to you, even if the specific Researcher role is currently closed.

I’m also working hard on my Japanese language skills in the meantime. I really appreciate you keeping me in the loop, and I look forward to hopefully joining the team in the future.

Best,

Yilei

***

### Key Phrases Used (Explanation):
*   **"I was recently away for the Chinese New Year holidays"**: Explains the "CNY" delay naturally.
*   **"Considering the hiring timeline and the strong alignment with my project interests..."**: Translates "入职周期 and 项目兴趣匹配" professionally.
*   **"I haven't had the bandwidth"**: A professional way to say you didn't have time.
*   **"Even though there are no immediate openings... I plan to complete the assignment... just for your reference"**: clear translation of your intent to do the test later despite the closed role.
*   **"Actively learning Japanese"**: Shows your commitment to the location requirements.
